comment_text.sn7.14:2.2: For bhava as “prosperity” see also Dhp 282:4.
comment_text.sn7.14:13.5: Note the difference: the brahmins actively seek a payment for teaching, whereas the Buddha receives only what is freely offered.

root_text.sn7.14:1.2: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇamahāsālo lūkho lūkhapāvuraṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn7.14:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ brāhmaṇamahāsālaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.14:1.6: Te maṁ dārehi sampuccha gharā nikkhāmentī”ti.
root_text.sn7.14:1.7: “Tena hi tvaṁ, brāhmaṇa, imā gāthāyo pariyāpuṇitvā sabhāyaṁ mahājanakāye sannipatite puttesu ca sannisinnesu bhāsassu:
root_text.sn7.14:7.1: Atha kho so brāhmaṇamahāsālo bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo pariyāpuṇitvā sabhāyaṁ mahājanakāye sannipatite puttesu ca sannisinnesu abhāsi:
root_text.sn7.14:13.1: Atha kho naṁ brāhmaṇamahāsālaṁ puttā gharaṁ netvā nhāpetvā paccekaṁ dussayugena acchādesuṁ.
root_text.sn7.14:13.2: Atha kho so brāhmaṇamahāsālo ekaṁ dussayugaṁ ādāya yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn7.14:13.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho brāhmaṇamahāsālo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.14:13.4: “mayaṁ, bho gotama, brāhmaṇā nāma ācariyassa ācariyadhanaṁ pariyesāma.
root_text.sn7.14:13.7: Atha kho so brāhmaṇamahāsālo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.14:13.9: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn7.14:1.2: Then a certain well-to-do brahmin, shabby, wearing a shabby cloak, went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn7.14:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn7.14:1.4: “Brahmin, why are you so shabby, wearing a shabby cloak?”
translation_text.sn7.14:1.6: At their wives’ bidding they expelled me from my house.”
translation_text.sn7.14:1.7: “Well then, brahmin, memorize these verses and recite them to your sons when you are all seated in the council hall with a large crowd.
translation_text.sn7.14:2.1: ‘I was overjoyed when they were born,
translation_text.sn7.14:2.3: But at their wives’ bidding they chased me out,
translation_text.sn7.14:3.1: It turns out they’re wicked, those nasty men,
translation_text.sn7.14:3.2: though they called me their dear old Dad.
translation_text.sn7.14:3.3: They’re monsters in the shape of sons,
translation_text.sn7.14:3.4: throwing me out as I’ve grown old.
translation_text.sn7.14:4.3: the elderly father of those kids
translation_text.sn7.14:4.4: begs for alms at others’ homes.
translation_text.sn7.14:5.3: for it wards off a wild bull,
translation_text.sn7.14:6.1: It goes before me in the dark;
translation_text.sn7.14:6.2: in deep waters it finds a footing.
translation_text.sn7.14:6.3: By the wonderful power of this staff,
translation_text.sn7.14:6.4: when I stumble, I stand firm again.’”
translation_text.sn7.14:7.1: Having memorized those verses in the Buddha’s presence, the brahmin recited them to his sons when they were all seated in the council hall with a large crowd. …
translation_text.sn7.14:13.1: Then the brahmin’s sons led him back home, bathed him, and each clothed him with a fine pair of garments.
translation_text.sn7.14:13.2: Then the brahmin, taking one pair of garments, went to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn7.14:13.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn7.14:13.4: “Worthy Gotama, we brahmins seek a fee for our tutor.
translation_text.sn7.14:13.5: May the worthy Gotama please accept my tutor’s fee!”
translation_text.sn7.14:13.6: So the Buddha accepted it out of sympathy.
translation_text.sn7.14:13.7: Then the well-to-do brahmin said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.14:13.9: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.sn7.14:1.2: lūkhapāvuraṇo → lūkhapāpuraṇo (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)

reference_text.sn7.14:1.1: csp1ed12.178, csp2ed12.178, ms12S1_1247, msdiv200, pts-vp-pli1ed1.176, pts-vp-pli2ed1.378, sya15.259
